name: Phase Diagram (Gibbs Phase Rule)
description: A phase diagram is constructed from the Gibbs phase rule F = C - P + 2, which fixes the degrees of freedom: single-phase regions and the boundaries between them are mapped on temperature-composition axes, the lever rule gives phase amounts inside two-phase regions, and invariant points complete the diagram.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["System: components C"]
    N2["Apply F = C - P + 2"]
    N3["Choose axes (T, comp)"]
    N4["Map single-phase regions"]
    N5["Locate phase boundaries"]
    N6{"In two-phase region?"}
    N7["Apply lever rule"]
    N8["Mark invariant points"]
    N9["Assemble phase diagram"]
    N10["Predict phases vs T,x"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|yes| N7
    N7 --> N9
    N6 -->|no| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
